data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The simultaneous collapse resulted from a critical threshold breach in the international tin-copper supply chain; the loss of a single, obscure tin source triggered a bronze shortage that paralyzed military and elite status systems.", "mechanism": "Supply chain cascade: The interruption of tin from a specific source (e.g., Afghanistan or Cornwall) halted bronze production, rendering chariot armies obsolete and undermining the material basis of elite power, leading to rapid political disintegration.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit"], "excludes": ["Egypt", "regions with local copper/tin sources"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Evidence of continued large-scale bronze weapon production and chariot deployment in collapsed regions after 1200 BC.", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Extends the supply chain failure model (artifact 83eb7701, b6371e69) by specifying the mechanism of military obsolescence."}
